01-01.title: Task Scheduler registration in postinstall-core.js
01-01.description: Export task name constant and registration/start helper functions for Windows Task Scheduler. Pure functions: build task parameters, return PowerShell command strings. Side effects isolated in callers.
acceptance_criteria: Task name constant exported from postinstall-core.js
Registration function creates Task Scheduler task at user logon trigger
Registration is idempotent -- updates existing task if present
Start function launches receiver and stops any prior instance
architectural_constraints: Pure functions: build task parameters, return PowerShell command strings
Binary paths in PowerShell commands must be properly escaped/quoted

01-02.title: Integrate registration into postinstall.js
01-02.description: Call registration and start functions from postinstall.js after binary installation. Non-fatal: try/catch around registration, install always succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: Postinstall registers Task Scheduler task after binary extraction
Postinstall starts hook receiver immediately after registration
Registration failure logs warning and does not fail install (exit 0)
Console output confirms registration and start on success
architectural_constraints: Non-fatal: try/catch around registration, install always succeeds

01-03.title: Integrate registration into install.ps1 and install.sh
01-03.description: Add Task Scheduler registration and immediate receiver start to standalone install scripts. Handle registration failure as non-fatal warning.
acceptance_criteria: install.ps1 registers task and starts receiver after binary extraction
install.sh registers task and starts receiver on Windows (win32 platform)
Both scripts handle registration failure as non-fatal warning
Re-running install updates task without creating duplicates
Running receiver stopped before binary overwrite, new instance started after
architectural_constraints: install.sh Windows path uses powershell.exe for Task Scheduler access
Task name matches constant from postinstall-core.js

02-01.title: Remove sidecar spawn and shell plugin from GUI
02-01.description: Delete sidecar spawning, remove tauri-plugin-shell dependency and all related config. No new dependencies introduced. Existing tray icon and window management behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: GUI starts without spawning hook receiver process
GUI displays data correctly regardless of receiver running state
architectural_constraints: Existing tray icon and window management behavior unchanged

quality_gates: No identical AC patterns across steps
